metadata.title:
GAS TANK EXPLOSION ON
ORIENTAL LIMITED EXPOSES
PATRONS TO DANGER AND
DISCOMFORT AT NIGHT IN
MOUNTAINS.

pages.raw_text:
The gas tank of the observation
car on the Great Northern Oriental
Limited train, which left Seattle at
$3.50 last evening, exploded when
the train was few miles west Of
Hot Springs, about 1880 this morn
ing. The car was considerably
damaged by the fire that followed,
and the passengers occupying the
sleeping compartments in it were
compelled to fee in their night
clothes to escape the flames and
gas. The car was Immediately cut
off from the rest of the train, and
the crew fought the flames with all
the available fire extinguishers,
with the result that the fire was
soon under control.


No one was seriously injured,
though one lady in her excitement
fell out of an upper berth and Sc
verely bumped her head. The pas
sengers in the observation car had
to climb out of the rear end Of the
car In their night clothing into
about six inches of snow and water
and walk two Car lengths to the
train. s


d. w. Twohy, president Of the
Old National bank, occupied a
berth next to the gas tank. He
